content:
🔥 tuitter | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Interacting with X (Twitter) content and features directly from the terminal.
✨ Key Features: • Terminal UI client for X (Twitter) • Authentication with X account • Browse and interact with content • Built with TypeScript and OpenTUI • Local token storage
📖 Summary: tuitter is a terminal UI client for X (formerly Twitter) built with TypeScript. It allows users to authenticate with their X account and interact with content directly from their command-line interface.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 caesium-app-web | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Compressing images offline and in the browser while maintaining user privacy.
✨ Key Features: • JPG, PNG and WebP supported • Lossy and lossless compression • Keeps images metadata • Before/after comparison • Runs entirely on your browser, no images are uploaded or processed on any external server
📖 Summary: Caesium Web Image Compressor is a privacy-focused, offline web application that compresses images directly in the browser using WebAssembly. It supports various image formats and compression types, ensuring no data is uploaded to external servers.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 zero_password_manager | Dart
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide users with a secure, private, and self-controlled method for storing and managing sensitive credentials and seed phrases, eliminating reliance on third-party cloud services.
✨ Key Features: • Open-source and self-hosted • Flutter-based application • AES-256-GCM end-to-end encryption • PBKDF2-SHA256 key derivation • Blind site hashing for URLs
📖 Summary: Zero Password Manager is an open-source, self-hosted password manager built with Flutter, prioritizing privacy and security. It allows users to store passwords and crypto seed phrases securely on their own servers, employing end-to-end encryption and zero-knowledge principles.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 rom | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: Deterministic web automation and browser API research
✨ Key Features: • Lightweight embedded JavaScript engine • Growing browser API compatibility layer • Deterministic probes, snapshots, and acceptance harnesses • JS and Python bindings • Focus on browser emulation
📖 Summary: ROM is a Rust-based browser runtime designed to be lightweight and programmable, emulating browser APIs without relying on Chromium. It's ideal for deterministic web automation, surface emulation, and browser API research.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
